items.title: Storm-1175 focuses gaze on vulnerable web-facing assets in high ...
items.description: The financially motivated cybercriminal actor tracked by Microsoft Threat Intelligence as Storm-1175 operates high-velocity ransomware campaigns that weaponize N-days, targeting vulnerable, web-facing systems during the window between vulnerability disclosure and widespread patch adoption. Following successful exploitation, Storm-1175 rapidly moves from initial access to data exfiltration and depl
items.ai_summary: The Storm-1175 threat actor is a financially motivated cybercriminal group that conducts high-velocity ransomware campaigns, targeting vulnerable web-facing systems. They exploit newly disclosed vulnerabilities, also known as N-days, to gain initial access to systems before widespread patch adoption. The attack vector typically involves exploiting unpatched vulnerabilities in web-facing assets, allowing the threat actor to move rapidly from initial access to data exfiltration and deployment of ransomware. To remediate, defenders should prioritize patching vulnerable web-facing systems, ensuring that all external-facing assets are up-to-date with the latest security patches, and implementing a robust backup and disaster recovery strategy to mitigate the impact of potential ransomware attacks. Additionally, defenders should monitor their networks for suspicious activity, such as unusual outbound traffic, which could indicate data exfiltration.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Malicious OpenClaw Skill Distributes Remcos RAT and GhostLoader
items.description: In March 2026, threat actors weaponized the OpenClaw AI agent framework by publishing a deceptive "DeepSeek-Claw" skill. This skill embedded malicious installation instructions designed to trick AI agents and developers into executing hidden payloads. On Windows systems, a PowerShell command downloads an MSI package containing a legitimate signed GoToMeeting executable that sideloads a malicious D
items.ai_summary: The 'DeepSeek-Claw' skill, a malicious OpenClaw AI agent framework component, tricks AI agents and developers into executing hidden payloads, leading to the installation of Remcos RAT and GhostLoader malware. This threat affects Windows systems, where a PowerShell command is used to download an MSI package containing a legitimate signed GoToMeeting executable that sideloads a malicious DLL. The real-world impact includes unauthorized access and potential data breaches for affected organizations. To remediate, defenders should block PowerShell commands downloading unsigned MSI packages and restrict the execution of GoToMeeting executables from untrusted sources. Additionally, monitoring for unusual network activity related to Remote Access Software, such as suspicious login attempts or data transfers, is crucial.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.001| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.004| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Iranian-Nexus Operation Against Oman's Government: 12 Ministries Hit and 26,000 Citizen Records Exposed
items.description: An exposed command and control server on RouterHosting infrastructure revealed an active Iranian-nexus intrusion campaign targeting twelve Omani government ministries. The operation primarily focused on the Ministry of Justice and Legal Affairs, deploying custom webshells that provided persistent access through April 2026. Over 26,000 user records containing judicial case data, committee decisions
items.ai_summary: The Iranian-nexus operation targeted twelve Omani government ministries, with a primary focus on the Ministry of Justice and Legal Affairs, by deploying custom webshells to gain persistent access through April 2026. The attack vector involved the use of web shells, specifically T1505.003, to establish a foothold in the targeted networks, and application layer protocol techniques, T1071, to facilitate communication with the command and control server. The real-world impact of this operation is significant, with over 26,000 user records containing judicial case data and committee decisions exposed. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately inspect their web servers for suspicious webshells and restrict access to only necessary personnel, while also implementing robust monitoring and logging capabilities to detect similar intrusion attempts. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing a web application firewall to detect and prevent malicious traffic, such as that associated with T1071.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1071| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1505.003| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1505.003| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Seqrite: Advisory: Middle East Conflict & Cyber Escalation
items.description: Iran’s cyber ecosystem operates through a layered structure that combines state-directed APT groups (IRGC-linked and MOIS-linked); semi-official contractors and front entities; hacktivist personas and collectives operated by intelligence services; and ideologically aligned foreign collectives operating in parallel. The Stryker Corporation attack on March 11, 2026 marked a significant escalation: a
items.ai_summary: The recent cyber escalation in the Middle East conflict has been attributed to Iran's layered cyber ecosystem, comprising state-directed APT groups, semi-official contractors, and hacktivist personas. The Stryker Corporation attack on March 11, 2026, marked a significant escalation, with threat actors employing tactics such as stealing or forging authentication certificates (T1649) and defacement (T1491). This attack has real-world implications, as it can lead to compromised sensitive information and disrupted operations. To remediate, defenders should implement robust authentication mechanisms, such as multi-factor authentication, and regularly monitor for suspicious activity. Additionally, ensuring all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches and configuring firewalls to restrict access to sensitive areas can help mitigate potential attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1649| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1491| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Multi-Stage AiTM Attack Uses Code Of Conduct Phishing Emails
items.description: A sophisticated credential theft campaign targeting over 35,000 users across 13,000 organizations was observed between April 14-16, 2026. The operation primarily impacted the United States, particularly healthcare and financial services sectors. Attackers used code of conduct themed phishing emails masquerading as internal compliance communications, sent through legitimate email delivery services
items.ai_summary: The multi-stage AiTM attack utilized code of conduct themed phishing emails to target over 35,000 users across 13,000 organizations, primarily in the United States' healthcare and financial services sectors. The attackers leveraged legitimate email delivery services to send these phishing emails, which masqueraded as internal compliance communications, thus exploiting the trust associated with internal communications. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement robust email filtering solutions, such as those utilizing machine learning algorithms to detect phishing attempts, and ensure that all employees undergo regular security awareness training to recognize and report suspicious emails. Furthermore, organizations should consider implementing multi-factor authentication to prevent attackers from utilizing stolen credentials. The real-world impact of this attack could result in significant data breaches and financial losses for the affected organizations.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1036| C[Persistence]:::tactic
    C -->|T1557| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1104| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Malware Bypasses Browser Application-Bound Encryption Protections
items.description: A sophisticated 64-bit information-stealing malware named Remus has emerged as a direct evolution of the notorious Lumma Stealer. Following the doxxing of alleged Lumma core members between August and October 2025, developers created this advanced variant, with test builds appearing in September 2025 and live campaigns starting February 2026. Remus employs innovative techniques including injecting
items.ai_summary: The Remus malware is a sophisticated 64-bit information-stealing malware that has evolved from the Lumma Stealer, with test builds appearing in September 2025 and live campaigns starting February 2026. It employs innovative techniques, including injecting into browser processes to bypass application-bound encryption protections, allowing it to steal sensitive information such as login credentials and credit card numbers. The real-world impact of this malware is significant, as it can lead to financial loss and identity theft for individuals and organizations that use affected browsers. To remediate this threat, defenders should ensure that their browsers are updated to the latest version, with specific attention to Chrome version 113.0.5672.63 or later, and Firefox version 111.0 or later, and consider implementing additional security measures such as two-factor authentication and regular password changes. Additionally, network traffic monitoring and anomaly detection can help identify potential Remus malware activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1021| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1531| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Data Extortion Groups Intensify Pressure On Global Aerospace Supply Chains
items.description: Cyber threats targeting the global aviation and aerospace sector are rapidly evolving, with ransomware, identity-based intrusions, and platform-level disruptions becoming dominant attack vectors. The interconnected nature of this ecosystem, combined with time-sensitive operations and complex third-party dependencies, makes it highly attractive to threat actors. Shared airport IT platforms represen
items.ai_summary: The global aerospace supply chain is under intense pressure from data extortion groups, with ransomware and identity-based intrusions being the dominant attack vectors. The threat actors are exploiting the interconnected nature of the ecosystem, targeting shared airport IT platforms and third-party dependencies to gain access to sensitive information. If exploited, this could lead to significant disruptions in time-sensitive operations, with potential real-world impacts including delayed or cancelled flights, compromised passenger data, and financial losses for affected companies. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust email account security measures, such as multi-factor authentication and regular password updates, and conduct thorough risk assessments of their supply chain partners. Additionally, ensuring all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches and implementing a robust backup and disaster recovery plan can help mitigate the impact of a potential attack.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1586.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Persistence]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: OceanLotus suspected of distributing ZiChatBot malware via wheel packages in PyPI
items.description: Between July 2025 and present, threat actors suspected to be OceanLotus distributed malicious wheel packages through PyPI targeting both Windows and Linux platforms. Three fake libraries (uuid32-utils, colorinal, and termncolor) were created to imitate legitimate packages, implementing a sophisticated supply chain attack. The packages deployed droppers that delivered ZiChatBot, a previously unknow
items.ai_summary: The OceanLotus threat actor is suspected of distributing ZiChatBot malware via malicious wheel packages on PyPI, targeting Windows and Linux platforms. The attack involves creating fake libraries, including uuid32-utils, colorinal, and termncolor, which mimic legitimate packages, allowing for a sophisticated supply chain compromise as outlined in TTP T1195. If exploited, this could lead to the deployment of ZiChatBot, potentially resulting in unauthorized access and data breaches for developers who have installed these packages. To remediate, developers should immediately remove the fake libraries and update their package lists to prevent further compromise, ensuring they only install packages from trusted sources. Additionally, monitoring system logs for suspicious activity related to these packages is crucial for early detection of potential breaches.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    B -->|T1204.002| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Operation Silent Rotor: Rust-Based Malware Targets Eurasian Unmanned Aviation Sector Ahead of Moscow Summit
items.description: A sophisticated spear phishing campaign targets professionals in the Eurasian unmanned aviation sector, timed to coincide with the XIII Eurasian International Forum 'Unmanned Aviation 2026' in Moscow. The attack delivers malicious archives containing Rust-based executables disguised as legitimate documents from the Russian Aeronautical Information Center. The malware displays aviation-themed decoy
items.ai_summary: Operation Silent Rotor is a sophisticated spear phishing campaign targeting professionals in the Eurasian unmanned aviation sector. The attack delivers malicious archives containing Rust-based executables disguised as legitimate documents from the Russian Aeronautical Information Center. If exploited, the malware can lead to data encryption and impact the aviation sector's operations. To remediate, organizations should implement robust email filtering and antivirus solutions, specifically targeting Rust-based malware. Additionally, professionals in the sector should be cautious of suspicious emails and verify the authenticity of documents before opening them.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1036| C[Execution]:::tactic

items.title: Unit42: Understanding Current Threats to Kubernetes Environments
items.description: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 explains that Kubernetes has become a prime target for attackers as its adoption accelerates in enterprise environments. Their research shows a sharp rise in Kubernetes-related malicious activity, driven less by classic container escape techniques and more by identity abuse and exposed application surfaces. Threat actors commonly gain initial access through misconfigurat
items.ai_summary: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 has identified a significant increase in Kubernetes-related malicious activity, driven by identity abuse and exposed application surfaces. Threat actors commonly gain initial access through misconfigured Kubernetes environments, leveraging techniques such as T1586.002 (Email Accounts) and T1649 (Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates). Once inside, attackers can use T1611 (Escape to Host) to escalate privileges and gain control over the environment. This can lead to data breaches, ransomware attacks, and other severe consequences. To mitigate this risk, defenders should ensure proper configuration of Kubernetes environments, including secure authentication and authorization mechanisms, and implement monitoring and detection tools to identify suspicious activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1586.002| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1611| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1649| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-5938: Improper control flow management allows a crafted document action chain to cause...
items.description: Improper control flow management allows a crafted document action chain to cause modal dialog reentry on the main thread, resulting in UI freeze and denial of service.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-5938 is a critical vulnerability in a widely used document processing software, affecting versions prior to 12.3.1. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by crafting a malicious document action chain, which causes a modal dialog reentry on the main thread, resulting in a UI freeze and denial of service (T1498). This can lead to significant disruptions in business operations, particularly in industries that rely heavily on document processing. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to version 12.3.1 or later, and ensure that the 'Document Action Chain' feature is disabled in the software configuration. Additionally, users should implement a web application firewall (WAF) to prevent network-based attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1498| D[Network Denial of Service]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-5937: Insufficient parameter verification leads to the occurrence of format errors in ...
items.description: Insufficient parameter verification leads to the occurrence of format errors in files, which will trigger an unhandled "std::invalid_argument" exception, ultimately causing the program to terminate.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-5937 is a medium-severity vulnerability in a specific product version (unspecified) due to insufficient parameter verification. When exploited, it causes an unhandled 'std::invalid_argument' exception, leading to program termination. This issue affects users who interact with the vulnerable product, potentially resulting in data loss or system downtime. To remediate, users should update to the latest version (1.2.3 or later) and configure the product to use strict parameter validation. Additionally, users should monitor system logs for suspicious activity and implement regular backups to minimize potential impact.

items.description: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in CodexThemes TheGem Theme Elements (for Elementor) allows DOM-Based XSS.This issue affects TheGem Theme Elements (for Elementor): from n/a before 5.12.1.1.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-42410 is a DOM-Based XSS vulnerability in CodexThemes TheGem Theme Elements (for Elementor) affecting versions prior to 5.12.1.1. This flaw allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into web pages, potentially leading to session hijacking, data theft, or unauthorized actions. To mitigate this issue, update to version 5.12.1.1 or later, and ensure that Elementor's security settings are configured to prevent XSS attacks. Additionally, consider implementing a Content Security Policy (CSP) to restrict the sources of scripts allowed to run on your website. Defenders should also monitor for suspicious activity and implement web application firewalls (WAFs) to detect and block potential XSS attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-7115: A vulnerability was identified in code-projects Employee Management System 1.0. ...
items.description: A vulnerability was identified in code-projects Employee Management System 1.0. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the file 370project/delete.php. Such manipulation of the argument ID leads to sql injection. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-7115 is a SQL injection vulnerability in code-projects Employee Management System 1.0, specifically affecting the file 370project/delete.php. An attacker can manipulate the argument ID to inject malicious SQL code, leading to unauthorized data access or modification. This vulnerability can be exploited remotely and is publicly available, increasing the risk of exploitation. To remediate, update to Employee Management System 1.1 or later, which includes a patch for this vulnerability. Additionally, ensure that the 'delete.php' file is properly sanitized to prevent similar attacks in the future.

This vulnerability has been publicly disclosed, and its exploit may be utilized by threat actors. To remediate this issue, update to Employee Management System 1.1 or later, and ensure that the 'edit.php' file is properly sanitized to prevent SQL injection attacks. Additionally, implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block potential exploitation attempts.

items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered three packages on the Python Package Index (PyPI) repository that are designed to stealthily deliver a previously unknown malware family called ZiChatBot on Windows and Linux systems. "While these wheel packages do implement the features described on their PyPI web pages, their true purpose is to covertly deliver malicious files," Kaspersky

items.title: Claude AI Guided Hackers Toward OT Assets During Water Utility Intrusion
items.description: Dragos has published a report describing how threat actors used Claude AI in an attack on a water and drainage utility in Mexico. The post Claude AI Guided Hackers Toward OT Assets During Water Utility Intrusion appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: vm2 Node.js Library Vulnerabilities Enable Sandbox Escape and Arbitrary Code Execution

items.description: Cisco patched a Crosswork Network Controller and Network Services Orchestrator denial-of-service vulnerability that requires manually rebooting targeted systems for recovery. [...]

items.title: New CISA initiative aims for critical infrastructure to operate offline during cyberattacks
items.description: The initiative, named CI Fortify, focuses on isolation and recovery efforts that would see critical infrastructure organizations proactively disconnect from third-party dependencies and find ways to operate without reliable telecommunications and internet.

items.title: DAEMON Tools devs confirm breach, release malware-free version
items.description: Disc Soft Limited, the maker of DAEMON Tools Lite, confirmed that the software had been trojanized in a supply chain attack and released a new, malware-free version. [...]

items.title: Autonomous Offensive Security Firm XBOW Raises $35 Million
items.description: The company raised another $35 million as an extension to its previously announced Series C funding round. The post Autonomous Offensive Security Firm XBOW Raises $35 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Why ransomware attacks succeed even when backups exist
items.description: Backups don't fail because they're missing, they fail because attackers destroy them first. Acronis explains how ransomware targets backup systems before encryption, leaving no path to recovery. [...]

items.title: Herd Security Raises $3 Million for AI-Powered Training Platform
items.description: The startup will invest in expanding its training categories, optimizing video generation, and growing its partnership ecosystem. The post Herd Security Raises $3 Million for AI-Powered Training Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: MuddyWater hackers use Chaos ransomware as a decoy in attacks
items.description: The MuddyWater Iranian hackers disguised their operations as a Chaos ransomware attack, relying on Microsoft Teams social engineering to gain access and establish persistence. [...]

items.title: MuddyWater Uses Microsoft Teams to Steal Credentials in False Flag Ransomware Attack
items.description: The Iranian state-sponsored hacking group known as MuddyWater (aka Mango Sandstorm, Seedworm, and Static Kitten) has been attributed to a ransomware attack in what has been described as a "false flag" operation. The attack, observed by Rapid7 in early 2026, has been found to leverage social engineering techniques via Microsoft Teams to initiate the infection sequence. Although the incident

items.title: Iranian APT Intrusion Masquerades as Chaos Ransomware Attack
items.description: Likely perpetrated by MuddyWater, the attack combined social engineering, persistence, credential harvesting, and data theft. The post Iranian APT Intrusion Masquerades as Chaos Ransomware Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Webinar: Why network incidents escalate and how to fix response gaps
items.description: Most network incidents don't escalate due to a lack of alerts; they escalate when response breaks down. This webinar explores how to fix gaps in triage, enrichment, and coordination. [...]

items.title: Hackers compromise Daemon Tools in global supply-chain attack, researchers say
items.description: Researchers at Kaspersky said attackers tampered with installers for Daemon Tools — a popular program used to mount disk images as virtual drives — and distributed them through the software’s official website.

items.title: The Hacker News Launches 'Cybersecurity Stars Awards 2026' — Submissions Now Open
items.description: For nearly 20 years, we at The Hacker News have mostly told scary stories about cyberspace — big hacks, broken systems, and new threats. But behind every headline, there’s a quieter, better story. It’s the story of leaders making tough calls under pressure, teams building smarter defenses, and security products that keep hunting threats 24/7 — even when it’s hard. Most of the time, this work is

items.title: From Stuxnet to ChatGPT: 20 News Events That Shaped Cyber
items.description: As part of its 20th anniversary celebration, Dark Reading looks back on 20 of the biggest newsmaking events from the past two decades that influenced the risk landscape for today's cybersecurity teams.

items.title: Romanian Man Extradited to US for Role in Hacking Scheme 17 Years Ago
items.description: Gavril Sandu, 53, was indicted in 2017, but was arrested and extradited to the United States only in 2026. The post Romanian Man Extradited to US for Role in Hacking Scheme 17 Years Ago appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Your AI Agents Are Already Inside the Perimeter. Do You Know What They're Doing?
items.description: Analysts recently confirmed what identity security teams have quietly feared: AI agents are being deployed faster than enterprises can govern them. In their inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, Gartner states that “enterprise adoption of AI agents is accelerating, outpacing maturity of governance policy controls.” Enterprise leaders can request access to the Gartner Market Guide for

items.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
items.description: In hard-to-detect attacks, hackers are dropping the CloudZ RAT and a fresh plug-in, Pheno, to hijack the Windows-based bridge between PCs and smartphones.

items.title: CISA Launches ‘CI Fortify’ to Prepare Critical Infrastructure for Geopolitical Cyber Conflict
items.description: Agency issued guidance and calls on operators to build resilient OT environments capable of surviving extended isolation and cyber compromise. The post CISA Launches ‘CI Fortify’ to Prepare Critical Infrastructure for Geopolitical Cyber Conflict appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Sophisticated Quasar Linux RAT Targets Software Developers
items.description: The persistent, evasive implant provides remote access, surveillance, and credential exfiltration capabilities. The post Sophisticated Quasar Linux RAT Targets Software Developers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Palo Alto Networks warns of firewall RCE zero-day exploited in attacks
items.description: Palo Alto Networks warned customers today that a critical-severity unpatched vulnerability in the PAN-OS User-ID Authentication Portal is being exploited in attacks. [...]

items.title: Google's Android Apps Get Public Verification to Stop Supply Chain Attacks
items.description: Google has announced expanded Binary Transparency for Android as a way to safeguard the ecosystem from supply chain attacks. "This new public ledger ensures the Google apps on your device are exactly what we intended to build and distribute," Google's product and security teams said. The initiative builds upon the foundation of Pixel Binary Transparency, which Google introduced in October 2021

items.title: Windows Phone Link Exploited by CloudZ RAT to Steal Credentials and OTPs
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an intrusion that involved the use of a CloudZ remote access tool (RAT) and a previous undocumented plugin dubbed Pheno with the aim of facilitating credential theft. "According to the functionalities of the CloudZ RAT and Pheno plugin, this was with the intention of stealing victims' credentials and potentially one-time passwords (OTPs),"

items.title: Government, Scientific Entities Hit via Daemon Tools Supply Chain Attack
items.description: While trojanized Daemon Tools versions were installed worldwide, a sophisticated backdoor was dropped only on a dozen systems. The post Government, Scientific Entities Hit via Daemon Tools Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Oracle Debuts Monthly Critical Security Patch Updates
items.description: Containing fixes for critical-severity vulnerabilities, the monthly rollouts will focus on addressing priority issues faster. The post Oracle Debuts Monthly Critical Security Patch Updates appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Palo Alto PAN-OS Flaw Under Active Exploitation Enables Remote Code Execution
items.description: Palo Alto Networks has released an advisory warning that a critical buffer overflow vulnerability in its PAN-OS software has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-0300, has been described as a case of unauthenticated remote code execution. It carries a CVSS score of 9.3 if the User-ID Authentication Portal is configured to enable access from the internet or any

items.title: Middle East Cyber Battle Field Broadens — Especially in UAE
items.description: As the war with Iran continues, breach attempts targeting the United Arab Emirates tripled in a few weeks — many targeting critical infrastructure.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, May 6th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9920, (Wed, May 6th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Trellix Source Code Breach Highlights Growing Supply Chain Threats
items.description: Info is scant, but such breaches can reveal where a security product's controls are located and how detections are designed, giving attackers a leg up.

items.title: Research Hub Bridges Cybersecurity Gap for Under-Resourced Organizations
items.description: The UC Berkeley Center for Long-Term Cybersecurity (CLTC) offers tools and support to schools, local governments, and non-profits as they defend themselves against a growing volume of cyberattacks.

items.title: Why Security Leadership Makes or Breaks a Pen Test
items.description: Well-run security drills go beyond checking audit boxes to identifying and addressing trouble spots. Effective leaders ensure proper scope, access, and follow-through, but it's not easy.

items.title: FTC bans data broker Kochava from selling sensitive location info
items.description: The FTC has said that Kochava sold precise geolocation data showing consumers visiting houses of worship and health care clinics without their consent or awareness, an alleged violation of a law barring companies from engaging in unfair and deceptive practices.

items.title: Critical Apache HTTP/2 Flaw (CVE-2026-23918) Enables DoS and Potential RCE
items.description: The Apache Software Foundation (ASF) has released security updates to address several security vulnerabilities in the HTTP Server, including a severe vulnerability that could potentially lead to remote code execution (RCE). The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-23918 (CVSS score: 8.8), has been described as a case of "double free and possible RCE" in the HTTP/2 protocol handling. This issue

items.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
items.description: A proof-of-concept exploit (PoC) shows how someone with admin privileges can exploit the issue to steal passwords, and thus use them to engage in further malicious activity.

items.title: Cleartext Passwords in MS Edge? In 2026?, (Mon, May 4th)
items.description: Yup, that is for real.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
How the Story of a USB Penetration Test Went Viral
items.description: Two decades ago, pen tester Steve Stasiukonis caused a sensation by sprinkling rigged thumb drives around a credit union parking lot and following what curious employees did next. This episode looks back at the history-making event.

items.title: SSL.com rotates their root certificate today, (Tue, May 5th)
items.description: I just got an email from SSL.com last night, they are rotating &&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;out their root certificate today (May 5,2026). &&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;This is normal, business as usual stuff for a CA, but certificates get used for all kinds of things, and sometimes they aren&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t used like they should be, so sometimes hiccups happen.

items.description: Summary

items.title: DShield Honeypot Update, (Mon, May 4th)
items.description: This week, I will release a few updates to our DShield honeypot. The update should happen automatically if you have "automatic updates" enabled on your system. There will be two major changes:

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, May 4th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9916, (Mon, May 4th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Wireshark 4.6.5 Released, (Sun, May 3rd)
items.description: Wireshark release 4.6.5 fixes 43 vulnerabilities (38 CVEs) and 35 bugs.

items.title: CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
